summary: Issue #421: weekly VPS-local restore drill (restore-drill.sh + systemd timer) proves juli-pre-migrate dumps are restorable. Scratch DB on same Postgres instance; backup filename resolved once at start; ADR-027 off-box constraint honored. Runbook documents schedule, logs, rotation guard, and failure triage.

interfaceChanges.description: pg_restore into scratch DB with row-count/token verification and journalctl pass/fail line.
Extends helper CLI for restore drill without changing production migration wrapper behavior.

mappings.criterion: Script runnable manually on throwaway Postgres creates a scratch DB restores into it verifies row counts and decrypt drops the scratch DB and exits correctly for pass cases
New integration test exercising dump restore into scratch DB verify drop including a case where the dump is intentionally corrupted and the drill must fail loudly
systemd unit files follow the same structure as infra/systemd/juli-api.service
Runbook documents drill schedule log location and the backup rotation race guard from the constraint

recommendations: Manual VPS systemctl start juli-restore-drill.service smoke remains HITL per issue test plan.
systemd-analyze verify on VPS when available.

reviewerSignoff.statement: Intent-review passed; Guardrails found no CRITICAL reliability or security gaps. Backup files never leave VPS per ADR-027.

ownerSignoff.statement: AC coverage mapped; integration and contract tests verified.
